content:
v1 accepted “42”. v2 accepted 42. Our API accepted neither from iOS.

6 min read
1 hour ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Friday 17:04 deploy. By 17:31 mobile crash rate hit 18%. Backend error rate: flat. Every /users/profile update returned 422 Unprocessable Entity for iOS users only.
Pydantic v2 did not break Python. It broke our assumption that query strings and form fields coerce the same way JSON bodies do.
Sixty-three thousand daily active users on builds we could not force-upgrade until Monday.
I led the pydantic bump PR. Green CI. Staging looked fine. Staging lied because QA only tested Android and web for that sprint.
The mobile payload archaeology
Charles Proxy export from support engineer’s phone — real device, real build 4.2.1:
POST /api/v1/users/profile HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencodeddisplay_name=Tu%C4%9Fce&age=&newsletter=1&tags=&legacy_avatar=Empty age. Empty tags. newsletter=1 not true — still coerced in v1.
iOS cleared numeric fields by sending empty string — UX pattern from 2019 UIKit form. Backend never documented that contract. Mobile team assumed JSON migration finished. It had not for profile edit.
The diff that looked like a patch bump
- pydantic==1.10.13
+ pydantic==2.6.4class ProfileUpdate(BaseModel):
age: Optional[int] = None
newsletter: bool = FalseMobile sent application/x-www-form-urlencoded:
age=&newsletter=true&tags=Pydantic v1: age="" → None. tags="" → [] after custom validator hack.
Pydantic v2 strict mode we enabled globally:
model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True)age="" → validation error. Not int, not null literal. 422.
Android sent JSON {"age": null} — fine. iOS legacy Alamofire layer sent empty string for cleared numeric fields — not fine.
v1 vs v2 coercion — what changed in practice
Not a feature list — the behaviors that burned us:

• Empty string to Optional[int] — v1 often coerced to None with loose config; v2 strict rejects

• tags as empty string — v1 list coercion from "" inconsistent; v2 wants JSON array or repeated keys

• newsletter=true string — both versions usually coerce to bool; until locale sent "True " with space — v2 trim behavior differed

• Union ordering — str | int validation order stricter in v2 for ambiguous inputs

• Extra fields — model_config extra='forbid' default shift in our codegen — mobile sent deprecated avatar_url — 422 not ignore
Comparison we should have run before Friday:
# migration test harness — run against golden mobile payloads
@pytest.mark.parametrize("payload", load_fixtures("ios_form_posts.json"))
def test_profile_update_accepts_legacy(payload):
r = client.post("/users/profile", data=payload)
assert r.status_code == 200, r.json()We had 200+ golden files from Charles Proxy exports. Ran them Monday. Should have been Thursday.
HTTP & REST API Reference — status code 422 vs 400, content negotiation, and why mobile clients mix form and JSON on the same route. The guide’s real scenarios include legacy client breakage on schema tightening.
The rollback that was not instant
Pydantic v2 sat inside FastAPI 0.110+. Downgrade chain:

• Revert pydantic pin — breaks fastapi starlette resolver

• Revert fastapi — breaks new lifespan hooks we shipped same sprint

• Full image rollback — 23 minutes canary to full fleet
Mitigation while rolled forward:
class ProfileUpdate(BaseModel):
model_config = ConfigDict(strict=False) age: Optional[int] = None @field_validator("age", mode="before")
@classmethod
def empty_str_to_none(cls, v):
if v == "" or v is None:
return None
return v @field_validator("tags", mode="before")
@classmethod
def tags_from_empty(cls, v):
if v == "":
return []
if isinstance(v, str):
return [t.strip() for t in v.split(",") if t.strip()]
return vPer-field validators everywhere we had mobile form posts. Ugly. Shipped in 41 minutes.
Migration report — what we’d do again
Week 1 — inventory

• Export production request samples (sanitized) per content-type

• Tag routes by client: ios_form, android_json, web_json

• Run bump-pydantic codemod on models — not enough alone
Week 2 — parallel validation
@router.post("/users/profile")
def update_profile(body: ProfileUpdate):
...@router.post("/users/profile/_validate_v2")
def shadow_validate(body: dict):
try:
ProfileUpdate.model_validate(body)
metrics.v2_validation_success.inc()
except ValidationError as e:
metrics.v2_validation_failure.labels(error=e.errors()[0]["type"]).inc()
return {"ok": True}Shadow endpoint in staging only — compare failure rates.
Week 3 — strict per model, not global
Global strict=True was convenience. Wrong convenience. Strict on new admin APIs; loose adapters on legacy mobile paths.
Week 4 — client fixes — iOS sends null not "". Backend keeps validators six months anyway.
OpenAPI and the false confidence
FastAPI regenerated OpenAPI — looked perfect. OpenAPI does not describe “iOS sends empty string for cleared int field.” Contract is bytes on the wire, not the schema PDF.
We added consumer-driven contract tests — mobile CI publishes fixture blobs; backend CI must accept them.
Honest limits
Pydantic v2 is faster and cleaner. The migration is not a version bump — it is a compatibility project. Budget eng-weeks, not eng-hours.
Validators are debt if they exist forever. Track each with ticket to remove when client min-version rises.
Friday deploys are a policy problem. We still ship Fridays. We also run golden mobile fixtures in CI on every pydantic minor now.
FastAPI-specific migration notes
FastAPI 0.100+ defaults to pydantic v2 models. Response model validation changed too — not just request body.
@router.get("/users/{id}", response_model=UserOut)
async def get_user(id: int):
...UserOut with model_config strict=True started rejecting ORM objects with lazy-loaded attributes in some edge cases. We saw 500 not 422 — different symptom, same migration.
model_validate vs parse_obj rename caught us in 14 internal scripts:
# before
User.parse_obj(data)
# after
User.model_validate(data)Codemod fixed most. Cron scripts on old VMs did not get deployed — silent batch job failures until Monday.
TypeAdapter for untyped dict endpoints
Legacy webhook route accepted raw dict:
from pydantic import TypeAdapterlegacy_adapter = TypeAdapter(ProfileUpdate)@router.post("/webhook/legacy")
async def legacy_hook(body: dict):
profile = legacy_adapter.validate_python(body, strict=False)Central place for loose validation without polluting every model.
Android vs iOS — same API, different bytes
Same endpoint. Two content types. Two client teams. One OpenAPI spec that described JSON only.
Android sends application/json — empty age is null. iOS legacy sends application/x-www-form-urlencoded — empty age is "".
No breakdown in dashboards at first — only in postmortem. We added client-family dimension to 422 metrics:
@app.exception_handler(RequestValidationError)
async def validation_handler(request: Request, exc: RequestValidationError):
client = request.headers.get("X-Client-Platform", "unknown")
metrics.validation_errors.labels(platform=client).inc()
...Friday spike was 100% ios_legacy. Would have narrowed RCA to 10 minutes with that label.
Communicating with mobile during the incident
We could not force app update until App Store review — 48–72 hours. Backend validators were the only fast lever. Technical debt accepted with expiry tickets per validator.
pydantic v2 Field constraints we missed
Field(gt=0) on optional age — empty string fails before coercion to None. Order matters: validator runs, then constraints. Our fix:
age: Optional[int] = Field(default=None, ge=0)@field_validator("age", mode="before")
@classmethod
def coerce_age(cls, v):
if v in ("", None):
return None
return vDocument every mode="before" validator with ticket link to remove when iOS min version 5.0 ships.
Three actions before next pydantic minor:

• Export real mobile payloads — not just OpenAPI examples

• Run uv lock --check equivalent for API contracts — golden files in CI

• Never enable global strict=True on a monolith serving legacy clients
Annotated types — pydantic v2 footgun #2
Annotated[int, Field(gt=0)] on optional field — empty string fails before Optional union resolves. We had three models with this pattern from codegen. Grep:
rg "Annotated\[.*Field" app/models/Review each against mobile fixtures.
If you are mid-migration: do not trust staging that only tests JSON from Postman.
Regression test we added to CI
tests/fixtures/ios_profile_form.txt — raw bytes from production. test_ios_legacy_profile_update posts exact bytes, expects 200. Runs on every PR touching ProfileUpdate model.
def test_ios_legacy_profile_update(client):
body = open("tests/fixtures/ios_profile_form.txt", "rb").read()
r = client.post("/api/v1/users/profile", content=body,
headers={"Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"})
assert r.status_code == 200One test. Would have saved Friday night. Cost 20 minutes to write Monday.
OpenAPI code generation trap
We generated TypeScript and Kotlin clients from OpenAPI — both assumed JSON. iOS hand-written layer still used form encoding. Generated clients masked how much traffic was legacy until crash rate split by User-Agent.
Add Content-Type breakdown to API metrics dashboard. Cheap. High signal.
Three actions before next pydantic minor:

• Export real mobile payloads — not just OpenAPI examples

• Run golden files in CI on every model change

• Never enable global strict=True on legacy client paths
Froquiz covers API design and Python typing edge cases in scenario form — useful when your schema change looks green in Swagger and red in the App Store crash dashboard.
→ Froquiz
